text: SESSION 2-JUL-2026: Architecture review. System assessment: bot_stable.py online, EventBus workers mismatch identified, duplicate polling resolved. Priority: STABLE CORE - single entrypoint, clean init, zero 409 by design. OPEN: create AGENT_RULES.md, populate journal, clean .bak files, verify bot.py dependencies, Dockerfile warnings, /refreshtoken decision, student onboarding.

NEXT SESSION TASKS: (1) Clean .bak files from disk. (2) Verify zero dependencies on bot.py, delete if clean. (3) Fix Dockerfile warnings. (4) Decide on /refreshtoken Railway auto-update. (5) Student onboarding - recruit 10 students. (6) smart_reply edge case - partial output before buttons.

SESSION 2-JUL-2026: Cleanup + hardening. DONE: AGENT_RULES.md created, journal initialized, 5 .bak files deleted, Dockerfile ENV BOT_TOKEN removed, .gitignore expanded. ISSUES: Railway usage limit reached (deploy blocked), token exposed in CLI history - must revoke. NEXT: replace token via @BotFather + Railway Dashboard, wait for usage reset, then deploy STABLE CORE.

SESSION 3-JUL-2026: Final stabilization. DONE: journal_read fixed (removed smart_reply), Dockerfile cleaned (ENV BOT_TOKEN removed), .gitignore expanded, old backup folders ignored, git status clean, /agent_create works in memory. OPEN: agents not persisted to db.json (volatile), /agents command hangs, start_agent_thread blocks import of bot_stable. NEXT SESSION PRIORITY: fix agent persistence to db.json, unblock /agents command, test full agent lifecycle. READY: Railway Online, Users:2, Deploy passes, security OK.

SESSION 3-JUL-2026 COMPLETED: agent persistence fixed (state/db.json + Volume), /exec runs Python, /demo with tasks/agents/guide, /inbox shows outbox, /start updated, state_manager centralizes DB access, tutor agent auto-replies. System ready for alpha users. Next: Ollama LLM integration, monetization plan.
SESSION COMPLETE 3-JUL-2026: fixed /start menu via welcome_handler.py, tutor worker replies, inbox shows outbox, /demo works, persistency stable. Ready for alpha + monetization.

SESSION 4-JUL-2026: Fixed slh_dashboard.sh token KeyError (options 1 and 2) - now falls back to BOT_TOKEN env var if db.json lacks token key, matches slh_daemon.sh pattern. Verified via live test + git push (2fb7af7). DISCOVERED RISK: dashboard health check uses pgrep -f python.*bot.py which never matches bot_stable.py, so it always reports Stopped and auto-triggers ./slh_daemon.sh - currently harmless because local bot_stable.py crashes immediately (ModuleNotFoundError: core.event_bus, plugins.task; No valid token found) but becomes a live 409-conflict generator if local deps ever get fixed. NEXT SESSION PRIORITY: fix dashboard pgrep pattern to match bot_stable.py, or remove auto-start entirely and just report status.

SESSION 4-JUL-2026 PART 2: Fixed dashboard auto-start risk - pgrep pattern now matches bot_stable.py (was matching bot.py, always false), removed automatic ./slh_daemon.sh launch entirely, relabeled as Local Bot to clarify it does not reflect Railway production status. Verified Railway container has zero local dependency on slh_dashboard.sh (only process running is bot_stable.py itself, no ps binary in container). Tested live - shows correct Not running status with no auto-launch. NOTE: dashboard DB health check (option 4) still checks stale schema fields (students, courses, admins, etc) that do not exist in current db.json schema (users, agents, tasks, memory, votes) - cosmetic, low priority, not fixed this session.
